Title: Data Manager
Description:
Job Title: Data Manager

Location: Reading (Hybrid - 2 days per week in office)

Industry: Digital Marketing

Salary: £40,000 - £50,000 DOE




The Opportunity

I’m currently working with a well-established Digital Marketing organisation based in Reading that is looking to hire a Data Manager to join its data function.




This is a key role within the business, focused on managing and optimising the flow, quality, and utilisation of campaign data across multiple client accounts and internal teams. The position sits at the centre of campaign delivery, ensuring data projects are executed effectively, reporting standards are maintained, and stakeholders have access to reliable, actionable insights.




You’ll work closely with teams across sales, client services, delivery, and operations, helping to drive successful campaign outcomes through strong data management, process improvement, and stakeholder collaboration. This is an excellent opportunity for someone who enjoys combining operational data management with commercial impact in a fast-paced, client-focused environment.




Key Responsibilities

Manage the end-to-end flow of campaign data across internal systems and client delivery functions
Coordinate and oversee data-related projects to ensure timely and accurate campaign execution
Maintain high standards of data quality, governance and reporting across multiple workstreams
Identify opportunities to improve data processes, workflows and operational efficiencies
Produce insights and reporting to support campaign performance and commercial decision-making
Work closely with internal stakeholders to ensure data requirements are understood and delivered effectively
Support data compliance and governance in line with GDPR and internal policies
Collaborate with client-facing teams to ensure successful onboarding, delivery and optimisation of campaigns
Act as a key point of contact for data-related queries, issues, and continuous improvement initiatives




Essential Skills & Experience

Previous experience in a Data Manager, Data Operations, Campaign Data or Project Management role
Experience within a B2B, marketing, technology or agency environment
Strong understanding of data workflows, reporting and quality management
Experience managing multiple projects or campaigns simultaneously
Excellent stakeholder and client management skills
Strong organisational skills with high attention to detail
Commercial awareness and ability to understand business and client objectives
Ability to work collaboratively across technical and non-technical teams




Desirable Skills

Understanding of data protection and GDPR regulations within the UK/EU
Experience with CRM systems and campaign management platforms
Exposure to data visualisation and reporting tools such as Power BI or Tableau
Experience improving operational processes or implementing workflow efficiencies
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills




Package & Benefits

£40,000 - £50,000 salary (depending on experience)
Hybrid working - 2 days per week in the Reading office
Performance-related bonus
Private healthcare
Pension scheme
Ongoing learning and development opportunities
Collaborative and commercially focused working environment

Title: Pricing Analyst - UK
Description:
Pricing Analyst | World of Books | London (Hybrid)

We're on a mission to become the Planet's Bookstore — and data is at the heart of how we get there.

World of Books is the world's largest seller of pre-loved books, with over 1,000 people across the UK, US and Hungary. We're a certified B Corp, and our proprietary pricing technology is core to how we serve millions of customers across 190+ countries. We're now looking for a Pricing Analyst to join our cross-functional Pricing Squad and help shape the next stage of our growth.

What you'll be doing

You'll own analytics and reporting that directly drives our pricing strategy — across both buying and selling. This is an end-to-end role: you'll build and validate datasets, design and run experiments, analyse simulations, and translate complex findings into clear commercial recommendations for senior stakeholders.

You'll be a key contributor as we transition to a new pricing system, running trials, sizing opportunities, and helping the business understand what's working and why. You'll partner closely with Commercial, Product, Tech, and Operations teams — and you'll have real visibility at a senior level.

What we're looking for

✔ 5+ years in analytics, ideally in eCommerce or marketplace environments

✔ Strong SQL skills — comfortable querying raw data in a Google Cloud environment

✔ Experience with BI tools (Tableau, Power BI or Looker)

✔ Python or R experience, ideally with Vertex AI

✔ Solid experiment design skills — A/B, multivariate, incrementality testing

✔ Degree in statistics, mathematics, computer science or related field

✔ Confident communicator across technical and non-technical audiences

Bonus points for experience in customer segmentation, LTV forecasting, or predictive modelling.

Why World of Books?

Every book we sell is one saved from landfill. We're a B Corp with genuine purpose at our core — and a business growing fast enough that your work will have real, visible impact. You'll join a collaborative analytics community that's raising the bar on data maturity, and you'll do it from our London office at least two days a week.

If you care about craft, commercial impact, and doing something that matters — we'd love to hear from you.

📍 London (hybrid, 2 days in office minimum)

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About Us – Fan Focused. Results Driven

At Gemba, we work at the intersection of sport, entertainment and culture, bringing strategy, data and analytics, insights and communications together to deliver results that matter.

Our name comes from the Japanese term gemba, meaning "the real place", which reflects our commitment to uncovering the truth at the heart of fan behaviour. Since 2006, we’ve built our reputation by understanding audiences deeply, helping our clients cut through the noise of an increasingly complex and competitive landscape.

We are proud to partner with major brands, rights holders and organisations to shape the future of sport and entertainment, whether driving commercial growth, increasing participation or creating meaningful engagement.

If you are passionate about fans, culture and creating impact, you’ll find your place at Gemba.

About The Role

We’re looking for a curious, detail-driven Analyst to join our Data & Analytics team. Someone who is passionate about turning numbers into narratives and building stories that clients can act on.

In this role, you’ll support in designing and delivering end-to-end analytics projects. You'll work across CRM, digital, social, and research datasets to unlock strategic insights that shape how brands connect with fans. As a trusted analytics partner, you’ll help clients understand their audiences and make smarter, data-led decisions.

This is a great opportunity for someone who enjoys hands-on analysis, wants to take ownership of deliverables, and is ready to help shape how brands connect with fans.

Responsibilities

Data Analysis & Visualisation


Query, clean, and transform data from multiple sources to communicate trends, behaviours and opportunities
Design and execute data analyses to support fan segmentation, campaign measurement, and business intelligence.
Develop scalable, intuitive dashboards and visualisations using tools like PowerBI, Tableau, or Looker.
Contribute to the development of advanced models and analytical frameworks (e.g. clustering, forecasting, propensity scoring).


Client Communication & Reporting


Work closely with internal teams and client stakeholders to translate findings into recommendations
Prepare clear, well-structured reports and documentation for both technical and non-technical audiences
Present findings with clarity and confidence, supporting client workshops and planning sessions


Team Collaboration & Innovation


Collaborate with Strategy, Creative, and Tech teams to deliver integrated project outcomes
Contribute to Gemba’s internal IP, case studies and thought leadership
Stay curious and always seek new ways to explore and present data


About You

Experience & Background


2+ years in a data analyst, marketing analyst, or BI analyst role
Strong technical foundation in SQL, Excel, and data visualisation tools (PowerBI, Looker, Tableau).
Tertiary qualifications in computer science, statistics, economics, mathematics, or similar
Familiarity with serverless workflows (e.g. Google Cloud Functions)
Interest in advanced analytics and ML techniques (segmentation, clustering, classification, propensity models)
A passion for sport, entertainment and the power of data to drive connection


Skills & Tools


Solid data manipulation, storytelling and problem-solving skills
Experience working with large datasets across CRM, digital, or marketing platforms
Strong communication skills and ability to simplify complexity
Ideally, exposure to Python, GCP, Git, or advanced analytics models


Success Looks Like


Insightful analysis that leads to clear, impactful client decisions
Outputs that feel effortless for clients to use and understand
A strong contributor to Gemba’s growing reputation in fan-first analytics


How To Apply

If you’re ready, click the Apply button below and submit your CV and cover letter. Address cover letters to Elaine Passanha, Senior People Manager

Note: All candidates require working rights of this country to perform the role.

Gemba is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We value diversity and encourage people of all different backgrounds, experiences, abilities and perspectives to apply. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, colour, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, disability status or other protected characteristics or similar.

Recruitment Agencies, we appreciate the interest, but we’ve got this one covered. Thanks!

At Gemba, we work at the intersection of sport, entertainment and culture, bringing strategy, data and analytics, insights and communications together to deliver results that matter.

Our name comes from the Japanese term gemba, meaning "the real place", which reflects our commitment to uncovering the truth at the heart of fan behaviour. Since 2006, we’ve built our reputation by understanding audiences deeply, helping our clients cut through the noise of an increasingly complex and competitive landscape.

We are proud to partner with major brands, rights holders and organisations to shape the future of sport and entertainment, whether driving commercial growth, increasing participation or creating meaningful engagement.

If you are passionate about fans, culture and creating impact, you’ll find your place at Gemba.

About The Role

We’re looking for a curious, detail-driven Analyst to join our Data & Analytics team. Someone who is passionate about turning numbers into narratives and building stories that clients can act on.

In this role, you’ll support in designing and delivering end-to-end analytics projects. You'll work across CRM, digital, social, and research datasets to unlock strategic insights that shape how brands connect with fans. As a trusted analytics partner, you’ll help clients understand their audiences and make smarter, data-led decisions.

This is a great opportunity for someone who enjoys hands-on analysis, wants to take ownership of deliverables, and is ready to help shape how brands connect with fans.

Responsibilities

Data Analysis & Visualisation

  • Query, clean, and transform data from multiple sources to communicate trends, behaviours and opportunities
  • Design and execute data analyses to support fan segmentation, campaign measurement, and business intelligence.
  • Develop scalable, intuitive dashboards and visualisations using tools like PowerBI, Tableau, or Looker.
  • Contribute to the development of advanced models and analytical frameworks (e.g. clustering, forecasting, propensity scoring).

Client Communication & Reporting

  • Work closely with internal teams and client stakeholders to translate findings into recommendations
  • Prepare clear, well-structured reports and documentation for both technical and non-technical audiences
  • Present findings with clarity and confidence, supporting client workshops and planning sessions

Team Collaboration & Innovation

  • Collaborate with Strategy, Creative, and Tech teams to deliver integrated project outcomes
  • Contribute to Gemba’s internal IP, case studies and thought leadership
  • Stay curious and always seek new ways to explore and present data

About You

Experience & Background

  • 2+ years in a data analyst, marketing analyst, or BI analyst role
  • Strong technical foundation in SQL, Excel, and data visualisation tools (PowerBI, Looker, Tableau).
  • Tertiary qualifications in computer science, statistics, economics, mathematics, or similar
  • Familiarity with serverless workflows (e.g. Google Cloud Functions)
  • Interest in advanced analytics and ML techniques (segmentation, clustering, classification, propensity models)
  • A passion for sport, entertainment and the power of data to drive connection

Skills & Tools

  • Solid data manipulation, storytelling and problem-solving skills
  • Experience working with large datasets across CRM, digital, or marketing platforms
  • Strong communication skills and ability to simplify complexity
  • Ideally, exposure to Python, GCP, Git, or advanced analytics models

Success Looks Like

  • Insightful analysis that leads to clear, impactful client decisions
  • Outputs that feel effortless for clients to use and understand
  • A strong contributor to Gemba’s growing reputation in fan-first analytics

How To Apply

If you’re ready, click the Apply button below and submit your CV and cover letter. Address cover letters to Elaine Passanha, Senior People Manager

Note: All candidates require working rights of this country to perform the role.

Gemba is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We value diversity and encourage people of all different backgrounds, experiences, abilities and perspectives to apply. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, colour, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, disability status or other protected characteristics or similar.

Recruitment Agencies, we appreciate the interest, but we’ve got this one covered. Thanks!

Title: Data Modeler
Description:
Supply Chain Model Builder




At Costa Coffee, we are what we craft. We’re reimagining coffee experiences in over 50 countries and counting, as a key part of the Coca-Cola System. Whether you get your coffee in a store, from a machine, at home, or on the go – we’ve got you covered.




This role supports the development and enhancement of Anaplan supply chain planning models, helping translate planning requirements into accurate, timely, and insightful tools while building specialist modelling skills through training and hands on experience.




Our teams make a difference. Whether that’s working on new tech for the perfect pour, helping our teams grow, creating award-winning campaigns, crunching the numbers, or developing the latest exciting menu item; together, we stir up success.




We may be a global brand, but we haven’t forgotten our roots. That’s where the Costa Foundation and our fantastic community agenda come in. Whatever your role, you can help us change lives in coffee growing communities and help your local community too.




We also want to help you grow in your career through amazing experiences, our apprenticeship scheme, and development programmes. At Costa, you can go beyond the day-to-day.

And as a Supply Chain Model Builder, there’s never been a better time to join.

So, why Costa?




We didn’t become a global coffee brand by sitting back. When you work here, you join a community that values passion, progression and integrity, with some pretty brilliant perks to sweeten the deal:

• Own a piece of Costa’s success by becoming a share owner in Coca-Cola with our Share

Investment Plan (SIP)

• An annual discretionary bonus scheme, based on business results and individual contribution.

• A smart pension that saves you money on tax and national insurance, and matches your

contributions up to 10%

• The Costa Financial Support Fund, supporting team members who find themselves in

unexpected financial pressure

• 50% discount in all Costa-owned stores, and 25% off in other participating stores

• Private medical cover thanks to our Private Healthcare scheme

• And that’s not all. Explore even more of our perks here: https://bit.ly/costaperks




We’re passionate about being a great place to work, where you can bring your unique self into our mix. We firmly support diversity, equity and inclusion, and continue to work with our teams to shape the future of our culture and values: Disciplined to Deliver, Passion for Progress, Win with Warmth, Courage to Challenge and Trusted Team Players.




What you’ll do:

Being a Supply Chain Model Builder is about so much more than bringing our coffee to the world. It’s your chance to stir up real success - which means you’ll expect to perform the following tasks:




• Translate business requirements into model designs




• Build and configure the models. This including the development of the data flows and

calculations as well as building the modules, UX pages and dashboards




• Validate, test and ensure data accuracy for all models




• Maintain and support models, including responding to/investigating user questions and

feedback




Who you are:




It’s your unique ingredients we’re interested in:




• Excellent data and analytical capabilities, with the ability to understand and build structured

planning models

• A detailed oriented person, focusing on and understanding the impacts of small changes on

downstream systems

• Iterative, problem-solving mindset

• Comfortable working with data, numbers and code

• Knowledge of supply chain processes would be an advantage

• A communicator that not only shares their ideas effectively, but can also interpret and

translate business requirements into technical plans

• Degree in Engineering, Data Analytics, Maths or a related discipline, or equivalent early-career

experience

• Graduate-level or entry-level experience in data analysis or model building is desirable




Where you’ll work:




Right now, our Support Centre teams work flexibly, blending home working with in-person time whenever it matters most – whether that’s a team moment, a creative session, or simply coming together to share ideas.




We’re excited to be moving into a new home for our brand in St Albans in January 2027 – an inspiring space from which our Support Centre teams will work three days a week to connect and collaborate in-person to bring our bold ambition to life.




For any reasonable adjustments and general queries, please get in touch with sarah.johnstone-cooke@costacoffee.com

Title: Yield Specialist
Description:
This role sits within Yield Management UK&I Region of our Commercial Division and reports into the Senior Yield Destination Manager

Have you ever wondered how the price of your package holiday is decided, why they sometimes change from the last time you looked or why the cost varies across different destinations?

Well, why not join our Yield department here at TUI to find out how it works in the world of travel! We are looking to recruit a team of Yield Specialists who get a buzz out of spotting patterns and love working with data to ensure a proactive approach to problem-solving and are commercially driven. You will have accountability for the revenue management of your own range of destinations, striving to optimise sales and profitability ensuring the right price is available to our customers at the right time.

We will ensure that you have first-class training and a support network of colleagues in place to help you succeed in the role. You’ll work through a development framework to support your long-term career goals, with progression opportunities available. A quote from one of our past specialists now in a manager role: ‘I really like being part of a big inclusive team and with a shared objective to sell as many seats/hotels at the best price. It means everyone really has to work together to get the best results'.


We offer flexible hybrid working, with one day a week on average in our Luton office to connect, collaborate and build strong relationships with the team. Brand new offices opening in Autumn 2026, close to M1 Junction 10 and Thameslink train line.


For those shortlisted to the final stage we will host in-person assessment days across May and June.

Now taking applications until 31st May 2026, please apply as soon as possible, as we expect a high volume of applications for this role. This may lead us to close the vacancy before the application closing date.

About Our Offer


Personal benefits: Salary £31,000 per annum, trading incentive schemes, generous travel benefits, extensive health & well-being support, and more.
Flexible hybrid working: Work is something you do, not somewhere you go. We encourage a healthy *work-life balance with a dynamic working environment.
A career to shape: Access the TUI Learning Hub to level-up and reach your ambitions.
Broaden your network: We champion intercultural collaboration and provide opportunities to work on global projects and teams
Community: Get involved with incredible local charity and sustainability initiatives like the TUI Care Foundation.


About The Job


You'll be working within a team environment responsible for several holiday destinations, striving to optimise sales, profitability and maximise both flight and hotel performance to hit key performance indicators.
In this analytical role, you'll use yield systems and review booking trends to make informed recommendations and decisions that deliver results in line with your destination strategy.
We'll rely on you to constantly monitor and react to demand in the competitive holiday market, offering relevant pricing solutions to maximise opportunities and overcome sales barriers.
Your decisions will directly drive the price offering to our customers and ultimately feed into the overall financial performance of TUI UK&I.
Building strong internal relationships with key stakeholders across the commercial division and beyond will be essential to drive current and future business strategy.
We offer a clear career pathway for progression, giving you the opportunity to drive your own development while learning alongside some of the best in the industry.


About You


Ideally, you’re have proven analytical experience, with the ability to interpret complex data and translate it into actionable insights.
Your numeracy and data analysis skills allow you to spot trends and make informed pricing decisions.
A structured, focused and conscientious approach to work ensures you deliver consistent results.
Your proactive, curious nature drives you to continuously learn and improve your understanding of the travel market.
Understanding different perspectives – both customers' and colleagues' – comes naturally to you, helping you make balanced decisions.
Highly proficient in Microsoft Office, especially Excel for data analysis and reporting


And of course, it helps if you love holidays as much as we do!

From a workplace to a place to belong. At TUI we embrace diversity, equity, and inclusion, encouraging everyone to come as you are, because together, our potential is limitless.

We are committed to supporting candidates with disabilities and impairments so if you require any support, please do let us know.

Title: IT Support & Data Systems Analyst
Description:
The Opportunity

I am partnering with a high-growth retail subsidiary of a major US PLC to find a versatile IT Support & Data Systems Analyst. This is a rare "hybrid" role for a technical professional who thrives on variety: balancing hands-on infrastructure support with a deep passion for Data Architecture and SQL.

As the second hire in a lean UK team, you will act as the digital "bridge" between warehouse operations and high-level financial reporting. You aren’t just fixing PCs; you are ensuring that data flows seamlessly from the stockroom to the boardroom.




Key Responsibilities

1. Data Engineering & SQL Management

Source of Truth: Manage and evolve data flows from on-prem SQL and Cloud resources.
Financial Automation: Work directly with the Finance Director to automate complex processes like revenue reconciliation, margin analysis, and marketplace settlements.
The Power BI Suite: Build and maintain sophisticated dashboards for senior management (DAX, data modelling, and visualisation).




2. Systems Integration & Digital Transformation

The "Google" Move: Support the business-wide transition from MS Stack/Excel to a Google Workspace environment.
Integration Management: Support the digital links between the ERP, Warehouse Management (WMS), and E-commerce platforms using APIs and basic scripting.
Infrastructure: Provide 1st–3rd line support across the business, covering everything from PC hardware and cabling to firewalls and backups.




What We Are Looking For

The SQL Core: You must have 2+ years of commercial experience with Microsoft SQL Server (querying, joins, and reading/writing stored procedures). This is a non-negotiable requirement.
Professional Maturity: At least 2 years in a professional corporate environment. You are a self-starter who doesn't need hand-holding.
Technical Versatility: A "start-up" mindset. You are equally comfortable configuring a firewall or troubleshooting a handheld scanner as you are building a complex data model.
The Stack: Familiarity with Azure Entra, Google Enterprise, and API-based integrations.
Domain Knowledge: Experience in Retail, Logistics, or Warehouse Management (WMS) is highly desirable.
Mobility: Willingness to work 4 days on-site across our Central London (Spitalfields) office and our Northamptonshire (Brackley) distribution centre.




The 6-Month Roadmap

Months 0–2: Deep dive into the ERP data model and lead the migration of legacy Excel workflows into Google Sheets/Cloud.
Months 3–5: Deliver "quick win" automations for carrier invoices to reduce manual finance workloads.
Month 6+: Document all system integrations to prepare the business for a future large-scale ERP transformation.




On Offer

Stability & Growth: A permanent role in a business backed by a US PLC, with a clear path toward data leadership.
Compensation: Competitive base salary + paid overtime.
Perks: 25 days holiday + your Birthday off, monthly staff lunches, and a generous friends/family discount scheme.
Well-being: Comprehensive counselling and support programs.




Interview Process

Stage 1: Virtual interview with the Head of IT Ops & Finance Director (Technical & Soft Skills).
Stage 2: Live SQL Test (Screen-shared) to verify your depth in querying and stored procedures.
Stage 3: In-person meeting with the MD to discuss culture, maturity, and long-term vision.

Title: Data Consultant
Place: City Of London, England, United Kingdom
Description:
Data Consultant (SAP Data Migration)



Location: Hybrid
Salary: Up to £50,000 + Bonus

We are currently supporting a growing consultancy that is looking to bring in a Data Consultant with strong technical foundations and the ambition to develop into a broader data-focused role over time.

This position will initially focus on supporting a large-scale SAP programme, working closely with business users on data migration activities, before expanding into wider areas including data analysis, governance, and transformation initiatives.

This is an excellent opportunity for someone looking to accelerate their career and gain exposure across both the technical and business sides of enterprise data projects.



What you'll be doing


Support data extraction and transformation activities from legacy systems into predefined templates
Work closely with business stakeholders to identify, resolve, and improve data quality issues
Support users through data validation cycles and Go-Live activities
Collaborate across business and project teams during SAP data migration initiatives
Gain exposure to broader areas including data governance, analysis, and transformation programmes


Requirements


2-5 years of experience within data-focused positions
Experience with Python, SQL, Power Query, or similar transferable technologies
Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills
Comfortable operating in business-facing environments
Analytical mindset with strong problem-solving skills


Desirable


SAP exposure
Data migration experience
Experience working on transformation or ERP-related projects

Package:

Salary up to £50,000
Bonus scheme
Clear development and progression opportunities

Title: Analytics Engineer
Description:
Where Growth has no Leash

Zigzag is the #1 app for dog training & life skills, helping pups and their pawrents build a lifelong bond. Too many dogs miss out on the right training, leading to behavioural issues or even abandonment. Our mission is to prevent that by making puppy training accessible and enjoyable for everyone.




We’ve been featured as Apple’s App of the Day, highlighted in New Apps We Love, and shortlisted for multiple industry awards. Backed by a major global petcare organisation (Purina), we’re a growing product-led company with big ambitions and a close-knit team.




What we are looking for

We are looking for a curious, impact-driven Analytics Engineer to join our lean data team. You will sit at the intersection of data engineering and business analysis, transforming raw data into reliable, scalable insights.




In this role, you will be the "builder and investigator." You will take ownership of the "last mile" of our analytics stack—crafting dbt models, defining metrics, and partnering directly with Product and Marketing stakeholders to answer critical business questions. You will work closely with our Data Lead, who will guide your technical growth.




We are also a highly forward-thinking team when it comes to artificial intelligence. We use AI tools daily to accelerate our data operations, and the data team plays a key role in supporting the development and quality assurance of new AI features within our app. If you love writing clean SQL, embrace modern AI-assisted workflows, and want to see the direct business impact of your work in a fast-paced startup environment, this role is for you.




You will

Build the Foundation: Develop, test, and maintain robust data models using dbt and BigQuery. You will transform messy, raw data into clean, business-ready datasets.
Investigate & Solve: Lead ad-hoc data investigations. When business metrics shift or anomalies appear, you will be the detective writing the SQL and building the dashboards to explain why.
Accelerate & Innovate: Actively use AI coding assistants and internal tools to speed up your development cycles, and collaborate with Product to monitor the quality and engagement of our app's AI features.
Empower Stakeholders: Translate complex stakeholder requirements into self-serve dashboards and semantic metrics using Lightdash and Mixpanel.
Data Quality: Implement dbt tests and maintain clear documentation to ensure our data remains trustworthy and accessible.
Collaborate & Learn: Work in a "guided autonomy" model. You will execute independently on data modeling and reporting tasks while receiving mentorship on advanced architecture from the Data Lead.




Your profile

Must-haves

Experience: 2+ years of experience in a data-heavy role (Data Engineer, Data Analyst, BI Analyst, or Junior Analytics Engineer).
SQL Mastery: You write clean, performant, and readable SQL (CTEs and window functions are second nature).
dbt Experience: Hands-on experience building and testing models in dbt (Core or Cloud). You understand the value of version control (Git) and modular data modeling.
Commercial Acumen: You care about the "So What?" You don't just build tables and dashboards; you want to understand how your data drives product engagement and marketing efficiency.
AI-Forward Mindset: You are genuinely excited about the practical applications of AI. You aren't afraid to use LLMs to augment your coding speed, and you are eager to analyse how users interact with AI in our product.
Communication: Strong ability to translate technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders and vice-versa.

Nice-to-haves (but not dealbreakers)

Experience with BigQuery.
Experience with semantic layer BI tools (like Lightdash or Looker) or product analytics tools (like Mixpanel).

HTML:
Where Growth has no Leash

Zigzag is the #1 app for dog training & life skills, helping pups and their pawrents build a lifelong bond. Too many dogs miss out on the right training, leading to behavioural issues or even abandonment. Our mission is to prevent that by making puppy training accessible and enjoyable for everyone.


We’ve been featured as Apple’s App of the Day, highlighted in New Apps We Love, and shortlisted for multiple industry awards. Backed by a major global petcare organisation (Purina), we’re a growing product-led company with big ambitions and a close-knit team.


What we are looking for

We are looking for a curious, impact-driven Analytics Engineer to join our lean data team. You will sit at the intersection of data engineering and business analysis, transforming raw data into reliable, scalable insights.


In this role, you will be the "builder and investigator." You will take ownership of the "last mile" of our analytics stack—crafting dbt models, defining metrics, and partnering directly with Product and Marketing stakeholders to answer critical business questions. You will work closely with our Data Lead, who will guide your technical growth.


We are also a highly forward-thinking team when it comes to artificial intelligence. We use AI tools daily to accelerate our data operations, and the data team plays a key role in supporting the development and quality assurance of new AI features within our app. If you love writing clean SQL, embrace modern AI-assisted workflows, and want to see the direct business impact of your work in a fast-paced startup environment, this role is for you.


You will

  • Build the Foundation: Develop, test, and maintain robust data models using dbt and BigQuery. You will transform messy, raw data into clean, business-ready datasets.
  • Investigate & Solve: Lead ad-hoc data investigations. When business metrics shift or anomalies appear, you will be the detective writing the SQL and building the dashboards to explain why.
  • Accelerate & Innovate: Actively use AI coding assistants and internal tools to speed up your development cycles, and collaborate with Product to monitor the quality and engagement of our app's AI features.
  • Empower Stakeholders: Translate complex stakeholder requirements into self-serve dashboards and semantic metrics using Lightdash and Mixpanel.
  • Data Quality: Implement dbt tests and maintain clear documentation to ensure our data remains trustworthy and accessible.
  • Collaborate & Learn: Work in a "guided autonomy" model. You will execute independently on data modeling and reporting tasks while receiving mentorship on advanced architecture from the Data Lead.


Your profile

Must-haves

  • Experience: 2+ years of experience in a data-heavy role (Data Engineer, Data Analyst, BI Analyst, or Junior Analytics Engineer).
  • SQL Mastery: You write clean, performant, and readable SQL (CTEs and window functions are second nature).
  • dbt Experience: Hands-on experience building and testing models in dbt (Core or Cloud). You understand the value of version control (Git) and modular data modeling.
  • Commercial Acumen: You care about the "So What?" You don't just build tables and dashboards; you want to understand how your data drives product engagement and marketing efficiency.
  • AI-Forward Mindset: You are genuinely excited about the practical applications of AI. You aren't afraid to use LLMs to augment your coding speed, and you are eager to analyse how users interact with AI in our product.
  • Communication: Strong ability to translate technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders and vice-versa.

Nice-to-haves (but not dealbreakers)

  • Experience with BigQuery.
  • Experience with semantic layer BI tools (like Lightdash or Looker) or product analytics tools (like Mixpanel).
  • Familiarity with Python (Pandas/scripting) for lightweight data manipulation.
  • Previous experience in a high-growth startup environment.


Why us?

Compensation & Rewards

  • Salary £45-55K pending on experience & interview performance, reviewed annually
  • Annual 10% bonus, increasing with tenure
  • Referral bonus


Wellbeing & Flexibility

Title: Experienced Data and Analytics Engineer
Description:
Company Description

At ZAIO Limited, we empower the future of telecommunications through data-driven innovations and agile software solutions. As a global IT and telecom solutions provider, we seamlessly integrate complex network infrastructure with high-performance digital operations. With decades of industry expertise, we support operators and enterprises throughout the entire network lifecycle by providing strategic consulting, advanced diagnostics, and tailored software tools.




Role Description

This is a full-time hybrid role for an Experienced Data and Analytics Engineer based in Newbury, Reading or Manchester with the flexibility to work remotely part of the week.




Role Purpose

We are looking for a hands-on Data & Analytics Lead to support the development and delivery of analytics and data engineering solutions within a telecommunications environment.

This role is suited to an experienced technical specialist who is comfortable working independently, building practical solutions, and supporting analytics delivery across operational and customer-focused use cases.

The successful candidate will have strong experience with cloud analytics platforms, Python development, SQL, and large-scale data processing, with a proactive mindset and willingness to take ownership of delivery.




Key Responsibilities

Data Engineering & Analytics

- Build and maintain analytics datasets, pipelines, and reporting solutions.

- Develop scalable ETL/ELT processes using cloud-native technologies.

- Support analysis of large datasets to generate operational and business insight.

- Work with structured and semi-structured data from multiple sources.

- Develop automated reporting and monitoring solutions.

GCP Platform Development

- Work within the Google Cloud ecosystem, including BigQuery, Cloud Storage, Vertex AI, Cloud Functions, and Dataflow.

- Support optimisation and maintenance of cloud-based analytics environments.

- Contribute to scalable and efficient data architecture design.

Cost & Performance Optimisation

- Help optimise BigQuery queries and cloud platform costs.

- Improve performance of data pipelines and analytics processes.

- Support implementation of efficient data processing approaches for large datasets.

Solution Delivery

- Translate business requirements into practical analytics solutions.

- Support rapid prototyping and development of analytics MVPs.

- Work closely with stakeholders across operational and technical teams.

- Produce clear documentation and maintain reusable code frameworks.

Telecommunications Analytics

- Support analytics use cases related to telecom networks, customer experience, and operational performance.

- Work with network KPIs, usage data, service quality metrics, and customer datasets.

- Assist in identifying trends, issues, and improvement opportunities from large-scale telecom data.




Required Skills & Experience

Technical Skills

- Strong experience with Python, SQL, BigQuery, and cloud-based analytics and data engineering.

- Good understanding of data pipelines, data modelling, query optimisation, and data transformation frameworks.

- Experience with data visualisation tools such as Tableau or Looker.

- Experience handling large datasets and automated workflows.

Experience

- Experience in data analytics, analytics engineering, or data engineering roles.

- Experience building solutions independently with limited supervision.

- Experience working in cloud analytics environments.

- Telecommunications or network analytics experience preferred.

Personal Attributes

- Self-motivated and proactive.

- Strong problem-solving mindset.

- Comfortable working in fast-paced environments.

- Able to independently manage and deliver technical tasks.

- Good communication and collaboration skills.

Desirable Skills

- Experience with AI/ML workflows.

- Exposure to customer experience or network analytics.

- Experience with operational analytics or monitoring solutions.

- Familiarity with Agile delivery environments.

Title: Pricing and Revenue Strategist (E-commerce)
Description:
About Us

Located in Milton Keynes, F&F Stores is a rapidly expanding E-commerce business that thrives on bringing licensed products from beloved brands like Disney, Marvel, Minecraft and iconic football clubs directly to your doorstep.

Our high-quality products include a wide range for the whole family, from toys and fashion to home accessories and beauty items, found on popular E-commerce marketplaces and our own e-commerce website.

Location: Office-based, in Milton Keynes

Contract: Full-time, Permanent

Number of vacancies: 3

Salary: £35,000 - £38,000

Role Overview

We’re looking for a Pricing and Revenue Strategist with strong analytical capabilities, financial insight, and deep ecommerce pricing experience — especially within Amazon or marketplace environments. In this role, you will drive revenue growth by developing pricing frameworks, optimizing pricing performance, and translating market data into actionable strategies that boost profitability and competitiveness.

Key Responsibilities

Strategic Pricing & Revenue


Lead analysis of market trends, competitor pricing behaviour, and customer demand to shape pricing strategy.
Propose, model, and optimise pricing recommendations that balance growth, margin, and market positioning.
Develop pricing frameworks, elasticity models, and scenario simulations to support decision-making.
Support the implementation and adjustment of dynamic pricing rules where applicable.


E-commerce & Marketplace Focus


Monitor pricing performance and key metrics across major ecommerce platforms, with specific emphasis on Amazon (Seller Central experience desirable).
Collaborate with marketplace operations, growth, and account management teams to align pricing with promotions, buy box priorities, and inventory strategies.


Data & Financial Analysis


Dive deep into sales performance, cost structures, and profitability modelling to inform pricing decisions.
Build dashboards, forecasting models, and reporting tools that provide insights into price elasticity and revenue impact.
Present findings and recommendations clearly to senior management and cross-functional partners.


Cross-Functional Collaboration


Work closely with Finance, Marketing, Product, and Supply teams to ensure pricing strategies support broader business goals.
Influence pricing decisions through data storytelling, clear communication, and strategic thinking.


Continuous Improvement


Monitor pricing performance post-changes and iterate on strategies to maximise business impact.
Stay current on ecommerce pricing trends, competitor moves, and technology enhancements.


Required Qualifications


Degree in Business, Economics, Finance, Data Analytics, or related field.
3+ years of experience in pricing strategy, revenue management, finance, data analysis, or related ecommerce strategy roles.
Strong analytical skills; ability to work with large datasets using Excel, SQL, BI tools, or similar.
Demonstrated ability to build pricing and revenue models and translate insights into business recommendations.
Excellent communication skills; comfortable presenting to stakeholders and leadership.


Desirable Experience


Hands-on experience with Amazon marketplace tools (Seller Central, pricing APIs, repricers, etc.).
Knowledge of dynamic and competitive pricing strategies in a marketplace environment.
Experience in BI or visualisation tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI).
Understanding of promotional strategies and their revenue impact.


Benefits


Casual dress
On-site parking
Regular social events including summer & end of year parties.
Spontaneous employee well-being treats
An Amazon Birthday voucher
Vouchers at Christmas to shop with F&F
Staff Purchases
NEST Pension
Private medical insurance with Vitality health with various benefits including discounts on a gym membership.

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
About Signal Ocean: Signal Ocean is the technology arm of the Signal Group. Our primary product, The Signal Ocean Platform, helps shipping and commodities professionals navigate their complex decision making. Driven by advanced machine learning and artificial intelligence, our technology suite provides tailored, exclusive insights that support our clients in achieving performance and efficiency. By securely handling and combining private and public shipping data flows, and applying advanced analytics, insights are delivered over web and mobile applications, as well as through a rich set of APIs and SDKs. Our backend architecture is abstracted to modularly offer deep analytics capabilities that are leveraged in the solutions that we offer or can be directly embedded in our client's system topologies.



About the role: 




We are looking for a Business Intelligence Analyst to join our growing Analytics team and help shape the next generation of shipping intelligence through AI-driven workflows.

In this role, you will transform raw data into actionable insights, build intuitive dashboards that support data-driven decision making, and work closely with product and commercial teams to better understand and address client needs.


As AI becomes increasingly embedded into analytics workflows, we are especially interested in candidates who are comfortable leveraging modern AI tools (e.g. Claude Code, Copilot, ChatGPT, etc.) to accelerate analysis, automate workflows, and create more intuitive and impactful analytical products. 


At Signal, we are committed to finding people who are respectful, inclusive, and team players, embodying these values in every role


What you will do in this role:



Analyze complex datasets to create meaningful data sources across diverse shipping topics
Leverage the latest AI tools to extract, analyze, and visualize actionable business insights
Build intuitive dashboards that deliver insightful storytelling and support strategic decision making
Translate client and business needs into analytical solutions and impactful reporting
Collaborate closely with product, data, engineering, and commercial teams
Use AI-powered tools to improve analytics workflows, dashboard creation, and insight generation
Identify trends, disruptions, and market patterns across shipping data
Contribute to the development of next-generation analytics products and reporting capabilities


Requirements



3+ years of experience in a Data Analyst / BI Analyst role
Strong analytical, problem solving and critical thinking skills
Hands-on experience with SQL and interpreting complex datasets 
Proven experience building intuitive dashboards and visual storytelling solutions
Practical experience leveraging AI tools and prompting techniques to deliver product features
Strong communication and teamwork skills


Strong plus but not required:

Ability to work cross-functionally with other product teams using Github and Jira 
Shipping or commodities industry knowledge



If you are passionate about analytics, AI-enabled workflows, and solving complex industry problems through data, we would love to hear from you.




Benefits



Generous compensation with additional performance incentives.
Coverage under the company's collective health insurance plan.
Opportunity to work alongside experienced people with deep knowledge in software engineering, data science & shipping business who are always eager to mentor.
Signal's hybrid remote work policy currently includes 9 working days at premises per month, during which happy hour events take place
2-4 weeks of onboarding training to prepare you for your new role, having the opportunity to meet about 30 trainers while diving deep into our products and/or the shipping world.
Career growth opportunities and a structured development discussion every 4 months.
Personal learning budget for training, seminars, conferences (750 to 2000 EUR annually depending on seniority).
Regular team bonding events and activities.


Strict adherence to Confidentiality, Intellectual Property and Non-Compete provisions is expected.

All applications will be considered under the terms and conditions of confidentiality in accordance with the regulations of personal data protection.

We are an Equal Opportunity Employer committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. At Signal, we believe that diversity of opinions, approaches and viewpoints is key to our innovation and success and we encourage that with our hiring, development and rewards practices. We prohibit discrimination and harassment of any kind based on race, color, sex, religion, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, genetic information, pregnancy, or any other protected characteristics by law and take actions to eliminate those from our workplace

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About Us – Fan Focused. Results Driven

At Gemba, we work at the intersection of sport, entertainment and culture, bringing strategy, data and analytics, insights and communications together to deliver results that matter.

Our name comes from the Japanese term gemba, meaning "the real place", which reflects our commitment to uncovering the truth at the heart of fan behaviour. Since 2006, we’ve built our reputation by understanding audiences deeply, helping our clients cut through the noise of an increasingly complex and competitive landscape.

We are proud to partner with major brands, rights holders and organisations to shape the future of sport and entertainment, whether driving commercial growth, increasing participation or creating meaningful engagement.

If you are passionate about fans, culture and creating impact, you’ll find your place at Gemba.

About The Role

We’re looking for a curious, detail-driven Analyst to join our Data & Analytics team. Someone who is passionate about turning numbers into narratives and building stories that clients can act on.

In this role, you’ll support in designing and delivering end-to-end analytics projects. You'll work across CRM, digital, social, and research datasets to unlock strategic insights that shape how brands connect with fans. As a trusted analytics partner, you’ll help clients understand their audiences and make smarter, data-led decisions.

This is a great opportunity for someone who enjoys hands-on analysis, wants to take ownership of deliverables, and is ready to help shape how brands connect with fans.

Responsibilities

Data Analysis & Visualisation


Query, clean, and transform data from multiple sources to communicate trends, behaviours and opportunities
Design and execute data analyses to support fan segmentation, campaign measurement, and business intelligence.
Develop scalable, intuitive dashboards and visualisations using tools like PowerBI, Tableau, or Looker.
Contribute to the development of advanced models and analytical frameworks (e.g. clustering, forecasting, propensity scoring).


Client Communication & Reporting


Work closely with internal teams and client stakeholders to translate findings into recommendations

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Merrifield Consultants is recruiting a Data Analyst for a respected professional body. This role sits in a small data insights team, producing evidence and analysis on areas such as membership, students, teachers, awards, research and the wider science landscape.




This is a fixed term contract until 31 December 2026, ideally starting as soon as possible.




Data Analyst

Salary: Between £34,800 to £36,800, pro rata (depending on experience)

Hours: 35 hours per week

Location: Mostly home based, with monthly team meetings, quarterly meetings and occasional office attendance when needed




The role will involve:

Working with messy data and turning it into clear, usable information
Using Python for data manipulation, analysis and reporting
Using Microsoft tools and AWS for access, storage and workflows
Producing clear visualisations and reports
Keeping KPIs up to date
Analysing internal data, including membership numbers, awards, students and teachers
Supporting general data analysis across projects




We are looking for someone with:

Good data theory and a sound understanding of data structures
Strong data manipulation and reporting experience
Confident Python skills
Experience producing clear visualisations
The ability to explain findings simply to different audiences
A careful, practical approach to data quality and accuracy




This would suit a Data Analyst who is confident with Python, understands how to work with imperfect data, and wants to work on useful evidence-led projects in a research, membership or not-for-profit setting.




For more information or to apply, please contact Akash Mahmud at Merrifield Consultants.




We practice a diverse and inclusive recruitment process that ensures equal opportunity for all we work with, irrespective of race, sexual orientation, mental or physical disability, age or gender. As an organisation, we encourage applications from all backgrounds and will ensure measures are met when required, to allow a fair process throughout.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Our purpose is to deliver energy to the world, today and tomorrow. For over 100 years, bp has focused on discovering, developing, and producing oil and gas in the nations where we operate. We are one of the few companies globally that can provide governments and customers with an integrated energy offering. Delivering our strategy sustainably is fundamental to achieving our ambition to be a net zero company by 2050 or sooner.

Job Summary

We are seeking a highly motivated Data Analyst to join BP Supply, Trading & Shipping. This role will be crucial in demonstrating data to drive informed commercial decisions, ensuring data quality, and contributing to our data strategy and governance initiatives.

Job Responsibilities


Perform comprehensive data analysis to identify trends, patterns, and insights that support commercial objectives.
Execute data cleansing and transformation processes to ensure data accuracy and consistency.
Integrate data from various sources, applying robust Extract, Transform, and Load (ETL) methodologies.
Develop and maintain data management practices to ensure data integrity and accessibility.
Manipulate large datasets to extract relevant information and support analytical requirements.
Proactively identify and source new data opportunities to enhance analytical capabilities.
Contribute to the development and implementation of BP's data strategy and governance frameworks.
Create compelling data visualizations and interpret complex data findings for diverse audiences.
Collaborate effectively in group problem-solving sessions to address data-related challenges.
Adhere to digital security protocols and best practices in all data handling activities.


Job Qualifications


Proven experience in data analysis, with a strong understanding of various analytical techniques.
Demonstrated commercial acumen and the ability to translate data insights into business value.
Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal, for presenting complex data clearly.
Proficiency in data cleansing and transformation tools and methodologies.
Strong data domain knowledge relevant to the energy sector (preferred).
Experience with data integration and ETL processes.
Solid understanding of data management principles and best practices.
Advanced skills in data manipulation using relevant software (e.g., SQL, Python, R).
Ability to effectively source and evaluate new data opportunities.
Familiarity with data strategy and governance concepts.
Expertise in data visualization tools and interpreting complex data outputs.
Awareness of digital security principles and data privacy regulations.
Strong collaborative and group problem-solving abilities.


Why join us?

At bp, we support our people to grow in a diverse and exciting environment. We believe that our team is strengthened by diversity.

There are many aspects of our employees’ lives that are meaningful, so we offer benefits to enable your work to fit with your life. These benefits can include flexible working options, a generous paid parental leave policy, excellent retirement benefits, among others!

We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us to request accommodation.

Reinvent your career as you help our business meet the challenges of the future. Apply now!

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Work where work matters.



We are a global technology group built for what's next, offering high calibre professionals the platform for high stakes work, the kind of work that defines an entire career. When you join us, you're not just taking on projects, you're solving problems that don't even have answers yet.

You will join an exclusive roster of talent that global leaders, including Google, Snap, Diageo, PayPal, and Jaguar Land Rover call when deadlines seem impossible, when others have already tried and failed, and when the solution absolutely has to work.  

Forget routine consultancy. You will operate where technology, design, and human behaviour meet to deliver tangible outcomes, fast. This is work that leaves a mark, work you'll be proud to tell your friends about.

We are looking for a Data Analyst with Looker Enterprise to analyse and define requirements, and then manage the end-to-end execution process. 

We look for people who embody:

Innovation to solve the hardest problems.

‍Accountability for every result.

‍Integrity always.

About The Role



This role is designed for impact, and we believe our best work happens when we connect. While we operate a flexible model, we expect you to spend regular quality time on site (at our offices or a client location) for collaboration sessions, customer meetings, and internal workshops.

Gain a deep understanding of the Looker BI Suite and Big Query, as well as surrounding GCP concepts such as IAM roles, Logging and Monitoring
Understand customer business requirements and advise the best way to fulfil these with the data and tools available
Investigate data necessary to achieve desired business outcomes
Concisely and thoroughly document processes relating to customer data requirements
Provide training and pair-programming sessions for clients to upskill them in using Looker and associated GCP services
Develop internal tooling, and improve processes, for the wider data analytics team and CTS business. You will love to find ways to do things better
As part of your continuing professional development, gain relevant certifications and skills badges as required by the role
We're proud of our B Corp status. We ask that you are proactive in our drive towards social progress and positive impacts. Whether that's getting involved in one of our community projects or simply buying local when travelling with the company. 


What Success Looks Like

Essential Experience:


Significant professional  experience working in a Data Analytics role
Professional  experience working with Looker Enterprise
Strong data processing experience and understanding of developing against common database technologies (such as MS SQL, PostgreSQL, MySQL, etc)
Comprehensive problem-solving skills and proven ability to apply creative and innovative thinking, identify and support the resolution of data quality and integrity issues
Understanding of developing visualisations and reports using BI solutions (such as Looker, Google Data Studio, Tableau, PowerBI, etc)
Confident in presenting findings in an effective, structured and actionable way to a wide set of audiences with varying levels of understanding
Excellent planning, prioritisation, organisational and time management skills, being able to work both as part of a team and independently 
Ability to meet regular and ad hoc deadlines, working under project plans and deadlines

Desirable Experience:


Exposure to data modelling / data science methodologies is a bonus
Prior experience working in a customer-facing role
Experience working in an Agile team, understanding of project management methods, disciplines and project lifecycles

Our Benefits



We believe in supporting our team members both professionally and personally. Here's how we invest in you:

Compensation and Financial Wellbeing

Competitive base salary
Matching pension scheme (up to 5%) from day one
Discretionary company bonus scheme
4 x annual salary Death in Service coverage from day one
Employee referral scheme
Tech Scheme

Health and Wellness

Private medical insurance from day one
Optical and dental cash back scheme
Help@Hand app: access to remote GPs, second opinions, mental health support, and physiotherapy
EAP service
Cycle to Work scheme

Work-Life Balance and Growth

36 days annual leave (inclusive of bank holidays)
An extra paid day off for your birthday
Ten paid learning days per year
Flexible working hours
Market-leading parental leave
Sabbatical leave (after five years)
Work from anywhere (up to 3 weeks per year)
Industry-recognised training and certifications
Bonusly employee recognition and rewards platform
Clear opportunities for career development
Length of Service Awards
Regular company events


Diversity and Inclusion



At Qodea, we champion diversity and inclusion. We believe that a career in IT should be open to everyone, regardless of race, ethnicity, gender, age, sexual orientation, disability, or neurotype. We value the unique talents and perspectives that each individual brings to our team, and we strive to create a fair and accessible hiring process for all.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About Us:

Solirius Reply, part of the Reply Group, delivers technical consultancy and application delivery to our clients in order to solve real world problems and allow our clients to respond to an ever-changing technical landscape. We partner closely with our clients, embedding our consultants into their businesses in order to provide a bespoke service, allowing us to truly understand our clients' needs.

It is this close collaboration with our clients that has enabled us to grow rapidly in recent years and will drive our ambitious future growth plans. We currently have over 300 consultants working with a variety of key clients from both the public and private sectors such as the Ministry of Justice, Department for Education, FCDOS, UEFA, International Olympic Committee and Mercedes Benz; with plans to increase our client base further in the near future.

We operate as a flat organisation and believe in trusting and supporting our team to operate independently. We pride ourselves on being specialists at what we do, making the most of our consultants' expertise in their fields in order to provide a best-in-class service to our clients. All our consultants have the opportunity to work on a range of different projects, providing a broad range of knowledge on which to develop their careers and progress in the direction they choose.

About You:

You are a motivated and adaptable professional with a strong analytical mindset and a passion for using technology to solve real-world problems. You enjoy working in collaborative, agile teams and take pride in delivering high-quality solutions that make a tangible impact. With strong communication skills and a consultative approach, you're comfortable engaging with clients, understanding their needs, and translating them into effective outcomes. You understand and align with Solirius Reply Values.

The Role:

We are looking for an experienced Data Analyst to join our team here at Solirius. You will be working as part of a team, developing and delivering exciting projects with a fantastic team of technology experts. You will be a fundamental part of the team and will be an expert in your field/specialism.

Requirements

Key Responsibilities:


Develop data solutions for our clients/projects
Diagnostics and Analysis for complex technical projects
Working collaboratively with the client and understanding their requirements
Working with a range of technical professionals to reach a desired outcome, including designing and delivering technical solutions


Key Skills/Experience:


Extensive experience of Data Analytics and Data Science with the ability to use BI tools like Power BI, Tableau etc.
Extensive knowledge of Performance Analytics and tooling such as Google Analytics, Google Tag Manager, Amplitude etc.
Experience in capturing requirements and Governance & Control documentation, knowledge of GDS is an added advantage
Applied experience of metadata analysis, fields/entity, and data lineage, sql/ no sql databases
Experience with one of Azure/AWS/GCP
ETL tools like Informatica, Alteryx and Business Glossary tools like Collibra, Azure Purview or Apache Atlas
TDD/BDD, Experience working in an Agile development environment
Identity management and synchronisation will be an add on for the resources
Experience with Data Dictionaries, Business glossaries would be an advantage


Benefits

Package and Benefits:


Competitive Salary
Bonus Scheme
Private Healthcare Insurance
25 Days Annual Leave + Bank Holidays
Up to 10 days allocated for development training per year
Enhanced Parental Leave
Paid Fertility Leave (5 Days)
Statutory & Contributory Pension
EAP with Help@Hand
Gym Membership Benefits
Flexible Working
Annual Away Days/Company Socials


Equality & Diversity:

Solirius Consulting is an equal opportunities employer. We are committed to creating a work environment that supports, celebrates, encourages, and respects all individuals and in which all processes are based on merit, competence and business needs. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, gender, sexuality, age, disability, ethnicity, marital status or any other protected characteristics.

Should you require further assistance or require any reasonable adjustments be put in place to better support your application process, please do not hesitate to raise this with us.

We operate as a flat organisation and believe in trusting and supporting our team to operate independently. We pride ourselves on being specialists at what we do, making the most of our consultants' expertise in their fields in order to provide a best-in-class service to our clients. All our consultants have the opportunity to work on a range of different projects, providing a broad range of knowledge on which to develop their careers and progress in the direction they choose.

About You:

You are a motivated and adaptable professional with a strong analytical mindset and a passion for using technology to solve real-world problems. You enjoy working in collaborative, agile teams and take pride in delivering high-quality solutions that make a tangible impact. With strong communication skills and a consultative approach, you're comfortable engaging with clients, understanding their needs, and translating them into effective outcomes. You understand and align with Solirius Reply Values.

The Role:

We are looking for an experienced Data Analyst to join our team here at Solirius. You will be working as part of a team, developing and delivering exciting projects with a fantastic team of technology experts. You will be a fundamental part of the team and will be an expert in your field/specialism.

Requirements

Key Responsibilities:

  • Develop data solutions for our clients/projects
  • Diagnostics and Analysis for complex technical projects
  • Working collaboratively with the client and understanding their requirements
  • Working with a range of technical professionals to reach a desired outcome, including designing and delivering technical solutions

Key Skills/Experience:

  • Extensive experience of Data Analytics and Data Science with the ability to use BI tools like Power BI, Tableau etc.
  • Extensive knowledge of Performance Analytics and tooling such as Google Analytics, Google Tag Manager, Amplitude etc.
  • Experience in capturing requirements and Governance & Control documentation, knowledge of GDS is an added advantage
  • Applied experience of metadata analysis, fields/entity, and data lineage, sql/ no sql databases
  • Experience with one of Azure/AWS/GCP
  • ETL tools like Informatica, Alteryx and Business Glossary tools like Collibra, Azure Purview or Apache Atlas
  • TDD/BDD, Experience working in an Agile development environment
  • Identity management and synchronisation will be an add on for the resources
  • Experience with Data Dictionaries, Business glossaries would be an advantage

Benefits

Package and Benefits:

  • Competitive Salary
  • Bonus Scheme
  • Private Healthcare Insurance
  • 25 Days Annual Leave + Bank Holidays
  • Up to 10 days allocated for development training per year
  • Enhanced Parental Leave
  • Paid Fertility Leave (5 Days)
  • Statutory & Contributory Pension
  • EAP with Help@Hand
  • Gym Membership Benefits
  • Flexible Working
  • Annual Away Days/Company Socials

Equality & Diversity:

Solirius Consulting is an equal opportunities employer. We are committed to creating a work environment that supports, celebrates, encourages, and respects all individuals and in which all processes are based on merit, competence and business needs. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, gender, sexuality, age, disability, ethnicity, marital status or any other protected characteristics.

Should you require further assistance or require any reasonable adjustments be put in place to better support your application process, please do not hesitate to raise this with us.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About Us – Fan Focused. Results Driven

At Gemba, we work at the intersection of sport, entertainment and culture, bringing strategy, data and analytics, insights and communications together to deliver results that matter.

Our name comes from the Japanese term gemba, meaning "the real place", which reflects our commitment to uncovering the truth at the heart of fan behaviour. Since 2006, we’ve built our reputation by understanding audiences deeply, helping our clients cut through the noise of an increasingly complex and competitive landscape.

We are proud to partner with major brands, rights holders and organisations to shape the future of sport and entertainment, whether driving commercial growth, increasing participation or creating meaningful engagement.

If you are passionate about fans, culture and creating impact, you’ll find your place at Gemba.

About The Role

We’re looking for a curious, detail-driven Analyst to join our Data & Analytics team. Someone who is passionate about turning numbers into narratives and building stories that clients can act on.

In this role, you’ll support in designing and delivering end-to-end analytics projects. You'll work across CRM, digital, social, and research datasets to unlock strategic insights that shape how brands connect with fans. As a trusted analytics partner, you’ll help clients understand their audiences and make smarter, data-led decisions.

This is a great opportunity for someone who enjoys hands-on analysis, wants to take ownership of deliverables, and is ready to help shape how brands connect with fans.

Responsibilities

Data Analysis & Visualisation


Query, clean, and transform data from multiple sources to communicate trends, behaviours and opportunities
Design and execute data analyses to support fan segmentation, campaign measurement, and business intelligence.
Develop scalable, intuitive dashboards and visualisations using tools like PowerBI, Tableau, or Looker.
Contribute to the development of advanced models and analytical frameworks (e.g. clustering, forecasting, propensity scoring).


Client Communication & Reporting


Work closely with internal teams and client stakeholders to translate findings into recommendations
Prepare clear, well-structured reports and documentation for both technical and non-technical audiences
Present findings with clarity and confidence, supporting client workshops and planning sessions


Team Collaboration & Innovation


Collaborate with Strategy, Creative, and Tech teams to deliver integrated project outcomes
Contribute to Gemba’s internal IP, case studies and thought leadership
Stay curious and always seek new ways to explore and present data


About You

Experience & Background


2+ years in a data analyst, marketing analyst, or BI analyst role
Strong technical foundation in SQL, Excel, and data visualisation tools (PowerBI, Looker, Tableau).
Tertiary qualifications in computer science, statistics, economics, mathematics, or similar
Familiarity with serverless workflows (e.g. Google Cloud Functions)
Interest in advanced analytics and ML techniques (segmentation, clustering, classification, propensity models)
A passion for sport, entertainment and the power of data to drive connection


Skills & Tools


Solid data manipulation, storytelling and problem-solving skills
Experience working with large datasets across CRM, digital, or marketing platforms
Strong communication skills and ability to simplify complexity
Ideally, exposure to Python, GCP, Git, or advanced analytics models


Success Looks Like


Insightful analysis that leads to clear, impactful client decisions
Outputs that feel effortless for clients to use and understand
A strong contributor to Gemba’s growing reputation in fan-first analytics


How To Apply

If you’re ready, click the Apply button below and submit your CV and cover letter. Address cover letters to Elaine Passanha, Senior People Manager

Note: All candidates require working rights of this country to perform the role.

Gemba is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We value diversity and encourage people of all different backgrounds, experiences, abilities and perspectives to apply. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, colour, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, disability status or other protected characteristics or similar.

Recruitment Agencies, we appreciate the interest, but we’ve got this one covered. Thanks!

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Merrifield Consultants is recruiting a Data Analyst for a respected professional body. This role sits in a small data insights team, producing evidence and analysis on areas such as membership, students, teachers, awards, research and the wider science landscape.




This is a fixed term contract until 31 December 2026, ideally starting as soon as possible.




Data Analyst

Salary: Between £34,800 to £36,800, pro rata (depending on experience)

Hours: 35 hours per week

Location: Mostly home based, with monthly team meetings, quarterly meetings and occasional office attendance when needed




The role will involve:

Working with messy data and turning it into clear, usable information
Using Python for data manipulation, analysis and reporting
Using Microsoft tools and AWS for access, storage and workflows
Producing clear visualisations and reports
Keeping KPIs up to date
Analysing internal data, including membership numbers, awards, students and teachers
Supporting general data analysis across projects




We are looking for someone with:

Good data theory and a sound understanding of data structures
Strong data manipulation and reporting experience
Confident Python skills
Experience producing clear visualisations
The ability to explain findings simply to different audiences
A careful, practical approach to data quality and accuracy




This would suit a Data Analyst who is confident with Python, understands how to work with imperfect data, and wants to work on useful evidence-led projects in a research, membership or not-for-profit setting.




For more information or to apply, please contact Akash Mahmud at Merrifield Consultants.




We practice a diverse and inclusive recruitment process that ensures equal opportunity for all we work with, irrespective of race, sexual orientation, mental or physical disability, age or gender. As an organisation, we encourage applications from all backgrounds and will ensure measures are met when required, to allow a fair process throughout.
